title: dryopteris marginalis
intro: Dryopteris marginalis, also known as the marginal wood fern, is a species of fern native to North America, characterized by its delicate fronds and unique marginal leaflets. The medicinal properties of the marginal wood fern lie in its ability to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of the marginal wood fern include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by infusing the dried leaves in hot water or mixing the plant's extract with other herbs and oils.
